name: Veal Ragout with Red Pepper
recipeIngredient: 3 pounds Veal stew meat, 1.5 inch cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat olive oil in a Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
2. Pat veal dry and add to the pan in batches. Cook until brown on all sides, adding more oil if necessary.
3. Drain on paper towels. Add carrots and onion to the pan and cook for about 5 minutes, stirring frequently.
4. Return veal to the pan, sprinkle with flour. Stir for 3 minutes.
5. Add stock and bring to a boil, scraping any browned bits from the bottom of the pan.
6. Mix in tomatoes, wine, parsley sprigs, crushed garlic, bay leaf (crumbled), thyme, basil (crumbled), salt, and pepper. Bring to a simmer, stirring constantly, and break up tomatoes.
7. Reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 1 hour. Uncover and simmer until veal is tender, about 20 minutes.
8. Add bell pepper and cook until tender, about 10 minutes. Transfer meat and bell pepper to a bowl using a slotted spoon. Discard parsley sprigs.
9. Skim surface of remaining liquid. Boil until reduced to about 2 cups, stirring occasionally, for about 20 minutes.
10. Return meat and peppers to the pan. Combine chopped parsley, green onion, minced garlic, and grated lemon peel.
11. Cook fettuccine in salted water al dente. Drain, return to pot, and mix with butter. Arrange around the outer edge of a large deep platter.
12. Spoon ragout into the center of the platter. Sprinkle with parsley mixture and serve.
